title: Make shallowCopy() not use a temporary image
body:
Modify `shallowCopy()` to not use a temporary image.
We assume that the big data items that we formerly added to the temporary image are small enough that we can just hang on to them.
We now write everything to the destination image instead of a temporary image, read it all back using the low level APIs, delete the image, and then recreate the image using the new layer and the saved items and names.
This lets us lift the requirement that we `shallowCopy()` only to images with names, so that `build-using-dockerfile` will work again when it's not being given tags.
